content.text: Answer seven problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

body.text:
Determine the Galois group of the polynomial
\[f(x)=x^4-14x^2+9\in\mathbb{Q}[x].\]

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Give the definition of a separable field extension.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that a finite separable extension is contained in a finite Galois extension.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Prove the Primitive Element Theorem: If \(E\) is a finite separable extension of \(F\), then \(E=F(\alpha)\) for some \(\alpha\in E\).

body.text: A pointed set is a pair \((S,x)\) with \(S\) a set and \(x\in S\). A morphism of pointed sets \((S,x)\to(S',x')\) is a function \(f:S\to S'\) such that \(f(x)=x'\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that pointed sets and their morphisms form a category.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that coproducts of arbitrarily indexed families of objects exist in this category.

body.text: Let \(F\) be the free group on \(n\) generators, \(n\geq 1\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that the automorphism group of \(F\) contains a subgroup isomorphic to the symmetric group \(S_n\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example of an automorphism of \(F\) that does not belong to this subgroup.

body.text: Define the term injective module for a ring \(R\) with 1. Prove Baer’s criterion: A unitary left module \(J\) is injective if and only if for every left ideal \(L\) of \(R\), any \(R\)-module homomorphism \(L\to J\) can be extended to an \(R\)-module homomorphism \(R\to J\).

Let \(R\) be a ring and suppose that we have a short exact sequence
\[0\to A\xrightarrow{f}B\xrightarrow{g}C\to 0\]
of left \(R\)-modules. Let \(M\) be a right \(R\)-module. Show that the induced sequence
\[M\otimes_R A\xrightarrow{1\otimes f}M\otimes_R B\xrightarrow{1\otimes g}M\otimes_R C\to 0\]
is exact.
Let \(R\) be a commutative ring with \(1\neq 0\) and let \(S\) be a multiplicative set containing 1.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: State the universal mapping property of the localization \(S^{-1}R\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that if \(S=R\setminus P\), the complement of a prime ideal \(P\), then \(S^{-1}R\) has a unique maximal ideal.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: \(R\) is an integrally closed integral domain.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Every nonzero prime ideal of \(R\) is maximal.

body.text: Let \(J\) be an ideal in a commutative ring \(R\) with 1. Assume that \(J\) is contained in every maximal ideal. Prove that if \(A\) is an \(R\)-module that satisfies the ascending chain condition on submodules, then \(\bigcap_{n=1}^{\infty}J^nA=0\).

Prove the Noether Normalization Lemma: Let \(R\) be an integral domain that is a finitely generated extension ring of a field \(K\). Let \(F\) be the field of quotients of \(R\) and let \(r\) be the transcendence degree of \(F\) over \(K\). Then there exist a subset of \(r\) elements \(t_1,\ldots,t_r\) which are algebraically independent over \(K\) and such that \(R\) is integral over \(K[t_1,\ldots,t_r]\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define the term left primitive ring and state Jacobson’s Density Theorem.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(R\) is a finite left primitive ring then \(R\cong M_n(F)\) for some finite field \(F\) and some natural number \(n\).
